  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Should I focus on (A) sentence-level writing polish and clarity only, (B) technical/argument review of the math and claims without rewriting, or (C) both?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "The paragraph makes specific mathematical and inferential claims (e.g., Gershgorin-based convergence, spectral radius, and conclusions) that warrant an internal logic and consistency check.",
    "R1.6": "As a standalone paragraph in a paper draft, sentence-level clarity, flow, and concision are likely primary concerns.",
    "RX.1": "Uncertainty should be flagged where claims depend on unverified assumptions or limited observation (e.g., convergence ranges and model families).",
    "RX.2": "The text includes potentially strong generalizations (e.g., 'broadly applicable,' 'drop-in without per-model calibration') that must be checked and tempered if unsupported.",
    "RX.3": "Feedback should remain scoped strictly to this paragraph rather than broader experimental design or the full paper.",
    "RX.4": "Discussion of bias estimation warrants care to avoid enabling misuse or overstating reliability in sensitive applications.",
    "RX.5": "Any feedback should reference specific sentences or statements to maintain traceability of critiques to the text."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Should I focus on (A) sentence-level writing polish and clarity only, (B) technical/argument review of the math and claims without rewriting, or (C) both?

rationale.R1.2: The paragraph makes specific mathematical and inferential claims (e.g., Gershgorin-based convergence, spectral radius, and conclusions) that warrant an internal logic and consistency check.
rationale.R1.6: As a standalone paragraph in a paper draft, sentence-level clarity, flow, and concision are likely primary concerns.
rationale.RX.1: Uncertainty should be flagged where claims depend on unverified assumptions or limited observation (e.g., convergence ranges and model families).
rationale.RX.2: The text includes potentially strong generalizations (e.g., 'broadly applicable,' 'drop-in without per-model calibration') that must be checked and tempered if unsupported.
rationale.RX.3: Feedback should remain scoped strictly to this paragraph rather than broader experimental design or the full paper.
rationale.RX.4: Discussion of bias estimation warrants care to avoid enabling misuse or overstating reliability in sensitive applications.
rationale.RX.5: Any feedback should reference specific sentences or statements to maintain traceability of critiques to the text.
